icpStages.Stage 1 – Awareness: Attract new eyeballs. Top-of-funnel. Broad crypto audience.
icpStages.Stage 2 – Education: Build trust via data-driven insights. Showcase frameworks.
icpStages.Stage 3 – Consideration: Position CS Platinum as the professional solution.
icpStages.Stage 4 – Conversion: Direct CTA to CWB event or Platinum signup.
icpStages.Stage 5 – Retention: Deepen engagement with existing members. Podcast, newsletter.

cards.title: LinkedIn Post — MVRV Bitcoin Bottom Signal

brief.hook: One indicator has called every single Bitcoin bottom. It is flashing again right now.
brief.angle: On-chain educational post. Explains MVRV ratio, historical bottoms (2012/2015/2020/2022), what the signal means today. Ends with save prompt.
brief.cta: Save this and come back in 6 months.
brief.copy:
One indicator has called every single Bitcoin bottom. It is flashing again right now.

The MVRV ratio triggered at every major low: 2012, 2015, 2020, 2022. We are approaching that zone again.

MVRV = Market Value to Realised Value. It compares Bitcoin's current price to the average price all holders actually paid.

When this drops to the bottom zone, the majority of holders are at a loss. That is historically when bottoms form. Long-term holders sell. New buyers step in. Those buyers hold until the market pushes back up.

One signal among many. But one of the most reliable in Bitcoin history.

Save this and come back in 6 months.

#Bitcoin #MVRV #OnChainAnalysis #BitcoinBottom #CollectiveShift

videos.title: How Influencers Actually Make Money (Trust & Incentives)

brief.hook: Most people don't realise how influencers actually make money in the digital asset space.
brief.angle: Educational breakdown of influencer revenue model (views, sponsorships, affiliate commissions). Conflict of interest framing. Ends with independent research as the alternative.
brief.cta: No CTA. Value post. Profile link does the work.
brief.copy:
Most people don't realise how influencers actually make money in the digital asset space.

There are three main revenue streams. Views, sponsorships, and affiliate commissions. The affiliate part is the one that should concern you.

When an influencer shares a link to an exchange or a platform, they earn a commission every time you sign up. Some of those deals pay per trade. Meaning the more you trade, the more they earn. They are literally incentivised by your activity, not your results.

In traditional finance, this is called a conflict of interest. If a financial advisor recommended stocks based on which company paid them the highest referral fee, they'd lose their licence. In digital assets, there's no licence to lose.

Here's the simple test I use. Look at how someone makes their money. If their revenue goes up when you trade more, they don't care whether you win or lose. They need you active. That's their business model.

Now compare that to independent research. A research firm makes money when you stay subscribed. You only stay subscribed if the research is good. The incentive is aligned with your outcome.

This is why I've always believed the research model is the only one that works long term for investors. Follow the incentive. It'll tell you everything you need to know about who to trust.

brief.copy:
Subject: How influencers actually make money (and why it matters to you)
Preview: Follow the incentive. It tells you everything.

Most people don't realise how influencers actually make money in the digital asset space.

There are three main revenue streams. Views, sponsorships, and affiliate commissions. The affiliate part is the one that should concern you.

When an influencer shares a link to an exchange or a platform, they earn a commission every time you sign up. Some of those deals pay per trade. Meaning the more you trade, the more they earn.

They are literally incentivised by your activity, not your results.

In traditional finance, this is called a conflict of interest. If a financial advisor recommended stocks based on which company paid them the highest referral fee, they'd lose their licence. In digital assets, there's no licence to lose.

Here's the simple test I use.

Look at how someone makes their money. If their revenue goes up when you trade more, they don't care whether you win or lose. They need you active. That's their business model.

Now compare that to independent research. A research firm makes money when you stay subscribed. You only stay subscribed if the research is good. The incentive is aligned with your outcome.
